name: Mock Crab Roe
recipeIngredient: 1/4 lb Fresh filet of sole, plaice or cod
2 Level tablespoons chopped spring onions (scallions), white parts only
recipeInstructions:
When the crab season ended, people were loath to give up the idea of being without crab. The following recipe is an excellent dish, but a poor substitute for the heady concentrate of roe found in the furry-clawed crabs of the Yangtse lakes.
1. Steam the fish for 10 minutes and flake it gently.
2. Mix the egg yolks together with the chopped spring onions, sesame oil, MSG, salt, and white pepper until well combined.
3. Add the flaked fish to the mixture and combine thoroughly.
4. Form small tablespoons of the mixture and fry in deep fat until the edges are nearly brown or pinkish gold. The fat will foam, but this cannot be helped.
5. Serve hot with a separate dish of soy sauce mixed with vinegar. Excellent with warmed wine.
From "Chinese Gastronomy" by Hsiang Ju Lin and Tsuifeng Lin, First Harvest/HBJ, New York, 1977. Introduction by Lin Yutang.
